rawText:
When Qatar's energy minister warned that the shutdown of his country's liquefied natural gas operations -- triggered by Iranian strikes on export facilities and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz -- would "bring down the economies of the world," he was not engaging in hyperbole, for Qatar supplies a fifth of the world's LNG, and its declaration of force majeure on contracts has sent prices ballooning across markets that supply electricity, heating, and fertiliser to billions of people.

The crisis has laid bare an uncomfortable hierarchy of suffering, in which the poorest nations absorb the greatest pain: Nepal has imposed cooking-gas rationing, Sri Lanka has urged firms to shut on Wednesdays, Pakistan has closed schools and moved universities online, and across the developing world, governments with thin foreign-exchange reserves and heavy dependence on Gulf energy imports are sliding toward the balance-of-payments crises that the IMF's director has called "the unthinkable."

Among the most vulnerable are Pakistan and Egypt, which spend three to four percent of GDP on oil and gas imports sourced predominantly from the Middle East while simultaneously depending on Gulf remittances worth five to six percent of GDP -- a dual exposure that means rising energy prices and disrupted labour markets can simultaneously widen current-account deficits and choke off the capital inflows needed to finance them.

Europe, which had only recently weaned itself off Russian gas by pivoting to American and Middle Eastern LNG after the 2022 invasion of Ukraine, now faces a second supply shock in half a decade, with natural-gas prices climbing back above fifty euros per megawatt-hour -- roughly five times the American price -- and the spectre of inflation returning to levels that could erode the real wages workers have only just recovered.

The notion that renewables might offer a swift escape from gas dependence, while appealing, founders on a stubborn physical reality: a grid that draws ninety percent of its energy from clean sources is not one in which fossil fuels provide a steady ten percent, but rather one in which, for a tenth of the time, nearly all power derives from gas -- because the sun does not always shine, the wind does not always blow, and battery technology cannot yet sustain grids through prolonged periods of renewable drought.

Even the best-case scenario, in which Donald Trump's "productive conversations" with Iran yield a ceasefire and Hormuz reopens within days, would leave global energy markets undersupplied for months, since Gulf producers need two to four weeks to restart wells shut down under unprecedented pressure, Qatar's damaged Ras Laffan liquefaction plant requires years of repair for its destroyed units, and the tanker fleet that once ferried Middle Eastern crude to Asia has scattered to the Atlantic on voyages that take up to ninety days to complete.

Countries with substantial reserves -- Thailand, with nearly a hundred days of strategic oil stocks and seven months of import cover, or India, whose refineries are configured to process discounted Russian crude and whose power grid runs largely on domestic coal -- stand a reasonable chance of weathering the storm, whereas nations like Bangladesh, whose garment factories run on imported fuel and whose reserves barely cover three months of imports, face the grim arithmetic of simultaneous shocks to both sides of the trade balance.

The scramble for alternative LNG supplies offers little consolation, because the world's export capacity is virtually tapped out -- Australia's producers run at ninety percent, America's at ninety-five -- and the only country with meaningful spare capacity, Russia, can deliver it mainly through pipelines to Europe, a solution that would require the EU to reverse sanctions, restart mothballed infrastructure, and once again entrust its energy security to the very power it spent four years trying to escape.

Europe's policymakers, caught between the imperative to lower energy costs for industries already losing ground to cheaper Chinese competitors and the need to preserve carbon-pricing mechanisms that drive the green transition, face a set of choices in which every path carries significant trade-offs -- extending free emission permits to energy-intensive firms undermines climate targets, while maintaining them risks accelerating the deindustrialisation of the continent's manufacturing base.

What the crisis ultimately reveals is that the global energy system, despite a decade of investment in renewables and diversification, remains hostage to a handful of physical chokepoints and geopolitical actors, and that the transition away from fossil fuels -- however necessary -- will not proceed quickly enough to spare the world from shocks whose humanitarian toll, measured in surging fertiliser prices, record hunger, and cascading financial crises across the developing world, falls heaviest on those least responsible for the conflicts that cause them.

overview.title: The Global Energy Crunch: Winners, Losers, and No Easy Exits

sentences.original: When Qatar's energy minister warned that the shutdown of his country's liquefied natural gas operations -- triggered by Iranian strikes on export facilities and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz -- would "bring down the economies of the world," he was not engaging in hyperbole, for Qatar supplies a fifth of the world's LNG, and its declaration of force majeure on contracts has sent prices ballooning across markets that supply electricity, heating, and fertiliser to billions of people.
sentences.translation: 카타르의 에너지 장관이 이란의 수출 시설 공격과 호르무즈 해협 봉쇄로 촉발된 자국 액화천연가스(LNG) 운영 중단이 "세계 경제를 무너뜨릴 것"이라고 경고했을 때, 그것은 과장이 아니었다. 카타르는 전 세계 LNG의 5분의 1을 공급하며, 계약에 대한 불가항력 선언은 수십억 명에게 전기와 난방, 비료를 공급하는 시장 전반에서 가격을 폭등시켰기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: When Qatar's energy minister warned that ...
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절 + that 명사절

grammarElements.text: triggered by Iranian strikes on export facilities and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz
grammarElements.label: 삽입 분사구 (과거분사)

grammarElements.text: he was not engaging in hyperbole
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사구 (과거진행 부정)

grammarElements.text: for Qatar supplies a fifth of the world's LNG
grammarElements.label: 이유를 나타내는 for절

grammarElements.text: its declaration of force majeure on contracts has sent prices ballooning
grammarElements.label: send + O + V-ing 사역구문

vocabulary.example: Calling it the worst crisis in history might sound like hyperbole, but the data supports it.
The supplier invoked force majeure after the earthquake destroyed its factory.

expressions.usage: 외부 충격이 가격을 걷잡을 수 없이 밀어올렸음을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Subject] was not engaging in hyperbole, for [factual reason]
patterns.explanation: 강한 주장이 과장이 아님을 사실적 근거와 함께 보여주는 패턴
examples: The CEO was not engaging in hyperbole, for the company had indeed doubled its revenue.
The scientist was not engaging in hyperbole, for the data showed an unprecedented rise.

drillQuestions.question: He was not engaging in ______, for Qatar supplies a fifth of the world's LNG.

sentences.notes: 한 문장 안에 시간절, 삽입구, 이유절, 사역구문이 모두 들어 있어 고급 문장 구조의 전형적 사례다. force majeure는 법률·경제 기사에서 자주 등장하는 프랑스어 차용 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: When Qatar's energy minister warned
the shutdown of his country's liquefied natural gas operations
he was not engaging in hyperbole
its declaration of force majeure on contracts
has sent prices ballooning

sentences.original: The crisis has laid bare an uncomfortable hierarchy of suffering, in which the poorest nations absorb the greatest pain: Nepal has imposed cooking-gas rationing, Sri Lanka has urged firms to shut on Wednesdays, Pakistan has closed schools and moved universities online, and across the developing world, governments with thin foreign-exchange reserves and heavy dependence on Gulf energy imports are sliding toward the balance-of-payments crises that the IMF's director has called "the unthinkable."
sentences.translation: 이번 위기는 가장 가난한 나라가 가장 큰 고통을 떠안는 불편한 고통의 위계를 적나라하게 드러냈다. 네팔은 취사용 가스 배급제를 시행했고, 스리랑카는 기업에 수요일 휴업을 권고했으며, 파키스탄은 학교를 폐쇄하고 대학을 온라인으로 전환했다. 개발도상국 전역에서 얇은 외환보유액과 걸프 에너지 수입에 대한 높은 의존도를 가진 정부들은 IMF 총재가 "상상할 수 없는 일"이라 부른 국제수지 위기를 향해 미끄러지고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The crisis has laid bare
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 + lay bare 구동사

grammarElements.text: an uncomfortable hierarchy of suffering
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: in which the poorest nations absorb the greatest pain
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (전치사 + which)

grammarElements.text: Nepal has imposed ... Sri Lanka has urged ... Pakistan has closed ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 병렬 예시절

vocabulary.example: The audit laid bare years of financial mismanagement.

expressions.usage: 한 나라의 외화 유출이 유입을 크게 초과해 대외 결제 능력이 붕괴되는 상황을 지칭

patterns.explanation: 사건이 불편한 현실을 드러낸 뒤 콜론으로 구체적 사례를 나열하는 패턴
examples: The pandemic laid bare the fragility of supply chains: factories stalled, ports jammed, and shelves emptied.
The scandal laid bare deep conflicts: allies split, donors fled, and voters turned away.

drillQuestions.question: The crisis has laid ______ an uncomfortable hierarchy of suffering.

sentences.notes: 콜론 뒤에 네 개 국가 사례를 빠르게 나열하며 위기의 규모를 보여주는 전형적 이코노미스트 스타일의 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The crisis has laid bare
an uncomfortable hierarchy of suffering
the poorest nations absorb the greatest pain
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 가난한 나라가 가장 큰 고통을 떠안는

koreanAlignment.en: are sliding toward the balance-of-payments crises

sentences.original: Among the most vulnerable are Pakistan and Egypt, which spend three to four percent of GDP on oil and gas imports sourced predominantly from the Middle East while simultaneously depending on Gulf remittances worth five to six percent of GDP -- a dual exposure that means rising energy prices and disrupted labour markets can simultaneously widen current-account deficits and choke off the capital inflows needed to finance them.
sentences.translation: 가장 취약한 나라 중에는 파키스탄과 이집트가 있는데, 이들은 GDP의 3~4%를 주로 중동에서 조달하는 석유·가스 수입에 쓰면서 동시에 GDP의 5~6%에 해당하는 걸프 송금에 의존하고 있다. 이 이중 노출은 에너지 가격 상승과 노동시장 교란이 경상수지 적자를 동시에 확대하면서 그 적자를 메울 자본 유입마저 차단할 수 있음을 뜻한다.

grammarElements.text: Among the most vulnerable are Pakistan and Egypt
grammarElements.label: 도치 구문 (장소부사구 + V + S)

grammarElements.text: which spend ... while simultaneously depending on ...
grammarElements.label: 관계절 내 while 동시 동작

grammarElements.text: a dual exposure that means ...
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격 명사구 + that절

grammarElements.text: can simultaneously widen ... and choke off ...
grammarElements.label: 병렬 동사

vocabulary.meaning: 해외 송금 (특히 해외 근로자가 본국에 보내는 돈)
vocabulary.example: Remittances from workers abroad account for a large share of the country's foreign income.

expressions.usage: 자본이나 공급의 흐름이 외부 요인에 의해 끊기는 상황에서 사용

patterns.pattern: Among the most [adjective] are [A] and [B], which [detail]
patterns.explanation: 도치 구문으로 가장 해당되는 대상을 강조하며 제시하는 패턴
examples: Among the most affected are hospitals and schools, which rely heavily on public funding.
Among the most promising are India and Vietnam, which have large young workforces.

drillQuestions.question: Among the most vulnerable ______ Pakistan and Egypt.

sentences.notes: 도치 구문(Among the most vulnerable are ...)으로 시작해 주어를 문장 뒤로 배치함으로써 취약성을 강조한다. dual exposure라는 표현이 두 가지 위험이 동시에 작용하는 구조를 명확히 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Among the most vulnerable
sourced predominantly from the Middle East
a dual exposure
choke off the capital inflows

sentences.original: Europe, which had only recently weaned itself off Russian gas by pivoting to American and Middle Eastern LNG after the 2022 invasion of Ukraine, now faces a second supply shock in half a decade, with natural-gas prices climbing back above fifty euros per megawatt-hour -- roughly five times the American price -- and the spectre of inflation returning to levels that could erode the real wages workers have only just recovered.
sentences.translation: 2022년 우크라이나 침공 이후 미국과 중동 LNG로 전환하며 러시아 가스 의존에서 겨우 벗어났던 유럽은 이제 5년 만에 두 번째 공급 충격에 직면하고 있다. 천연가스 가격은 메가와트시당 50유로를 다시 넘어섰고 — 이는 미국 가격의 대략 5배다 — 노동자들이 이제 막 회복한 실질임금을 잠식할 수준으로 인플레이션이 되돌아올 조짐이 나타나고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Europe, which had only recently weaned itself off Russian gas
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 삽입 관계절 (과거완료)

grammarElements.text: by pivoting to American and Middle Eastern LNG
grammarElements.label: 수단을 나타내는 by + 동명사

grammarElements.text: now faces a second supply shock
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사

grammarElements.text: with natural-gas prices climbing back above fifty euros
grammarElements.label: with + O + V-ing 부대상황

vocabulary.example: The country has struggled to wean itself off cheap coal.

expressions.usage: 과거의 위협이 다시 현실화될 가능성이 있을 때 사용하는 비유적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Entity], which had only recently [achievement], now faces [new threat]
patterns.explanation: 최근의 성과가 새로운 위기에 의해 무색해지는 상황을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The firm, which had only recently returned to profitability, now faces a hostile takeover.
The region, which had only recently recovered from drought, now faces severe flooding.

drillQuestions.question: Europe, which had only recently ______ itself off Russian gas, now faces a second supply shock.

sentences.notes: 과거완료(had weaned)와 현재시제(now faces)의 대비가 유럽이 한 위기에서 벗어나자마자 다른 위기를 맞이하는 아이러니를 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: which had only recently weaned itself off Russian gas
by pivoting to American and Middle Eastern LNG
now faces a second supply shock
koreanAlignment.ko: 이제 두 번째 공급 충격에 직면하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the spectre of inflation returning

pronunciationNotes: spectre는 영국식 철자 — 미국식은 specter, 발음은 /ˈspektər/

sentences.original: The notion that renewables might offer a swift escape from gas dependence, while appealing, founders on a stubborn physical reality: a grid that draws ninety percent of its energy from clean sources is not one in which fossil fuels provide a steady ten percent, but rather one in which, for a tenth of the time, nearly all power derives from gas -- because the sun does not always shine, the wind does not always blow, and battery technology cannot yet sustain grids through prolonged periods of renewable drought.
sentences.translation: 재생에너지가 가스 의존에서 빠르게 탈출할 수 있다는 생각은 매력적이기는 하지만, 완고한 물리적 현실에 부딪혀 좌초한다. 에너지의 90%를 청정 에너지에서 끌어오는 전력망이란 화석연료가 꾸준히 10%를 채우는 것이 아니라, 전체 시간의 10분의 1 동안 거의 모든 전력이 가스에서 나오는 것이다 — 해가 항상 비치지 않고, 바람이 항상 불지 않으며, 배터리 기술은 아직 장기간의 재생에너지 가뭄을 견딜 수 없기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: The notion that renewables might offer a swift escape
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격 that절

grammarElements.text: while appealing
grammarElements.label: 양보 분사구

grammarElements.text: founders on a stubborn physical reality
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사 (founder on = 좌초하다)

grammarElements.text: not one in which ... but rather one in which ...
grammarElements.label: not A but rather B 대조 구조

vocabulary.example: The peace talks foundered on mutual distrust.
Northern Europe experienced a two-week renewable drought last winter.

expressions.usage: 통념을 뒤집고 실제 상황을 제시할 때 쓰는 대조 표현

patterns.pattern: The notion that [hopeful idea], while appealing, founders on [harsh reality]
patterns.explanation: 매력적인 아이디어가 현실의 벽에 부딪히는 구조를 보여주는 패턴
examples: The notion that tax cuts pay for themselves, while appealing, founders on fiscal arithmetic.
The notion that remote work eliminates the need for offices, while appealing, founders on the reality of collaboration.

drillQuestions.question: The notion that renewables might offer a swift escape, while appealing, ______ on a stubborn physical reality.

sentences.notes: founder는 '설립자(founder)'와 철자가 같지만 동사로는 '좌초하다'를 뜻한다. not ... but rather ... 구조로 재생에너지에 대한 통념을 정면으로 뒤집는다.

koreanAlignment.en: The notion that renewables might offer a swift escape
koreanAlignment.ko: 재생에너지가 빠르게 탈출할 수 있다는 생각은

koreanAlignment.en: while appealing
founders on a stubborn physical reality
the sun does not always shine, the wind does not always blow
koreanAlignment.ko: 해가 항상 비치지 않고 바람이 항상 불지 않으며

pronunciationNotes: founders(동사)는 /ˈfaʊndərz/ — founder(설립자)와 발음 동일하나 의미가 전혀 다름

sentences.original: Even the best-case scenario, in which Donald Trump's "productive conversations" with Iran yield a ceasefire and Hormuz reopens within days, would leave global energy markets undersupplied for months, since Gulf producers need two to four weeks to restart wells shut down under unprecedented pressure, Qatar's damaged Ras Laffan liquefaction plant requires years of repair for its destroyed units, and the tanker fleet that once ferried Middle Eastern crude to Asia has scattered to the Atlantic on voyages that take up to ninety days to complete.
sentences.translation: 트럼프의 이란과의 "생산적 대화"가 휴전으로 이어지고 호르무즈 해협이 며칠 안에 재개방되는 최선의 시나리오에서조차 세계 에너지 시장은 수개월간 공급 부족 상태에 놓일 것이다. 걸프 산유국들이 전례 없는 압력하에 폐쇄된 유정을 재가동하는 데 2~4주가 필요하고, 카타르의 손상된 라스라판 액화 시설은 파괴된 설비 수리에 수년이 걸리며, 한때 중동 원유를 아시아로 실어 나르던 유조선단은 최대 90일이 걸리는 항로를 타고 대서양으로 흩어져 버렸기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: Even the best-case scenario
grammarElements.label: 주어 (even으로 양보 강조)

grammarElements.text: in which Donald Trump's ... conversations ... yield a ceasefire
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (시나리오 내용 설명)

grammarElements.text: would leave global energy markets undersupplied
grammarElements.label: 가정법 주절 동사 (leave + O + C)

grammarElements.text: since Gulf producers need ... Qatar's damaged ... requires ... and the tanker fleet ... has scattered ...
grammarElements.label: since절 내 세 가지 이유 병렬

vocabulary.example: The market remained undersupplied even after emergency reserves were released.

expressions.usage: 가장 낙관적인 전망도 충분하지 않음을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Even the best-case scenario ... would leave [situation] [negative state], since [reason 1], [reason 2], and [reason 3]
patterns.explanation: 최선의 경우에도 부정적 결과가 불가피함을 세 가지 이유로 뒷받침하는 패턴
examples: Even the best-case scenario would leave the budget in deficit, since revenues are falling, spending is locked in, and borrowing costs are rising.
Even the best-case scenario would leave hospitals overstretched, since staff are burned out, beds are full, and funding has not kept pace.

drillQuestions.question: Even the best-case scenario would leave global energy markets ______ for months.

sentences.notes: Even으로 시작해 최선의 경우조차 비관적임을 보여준 뒤, since절 안에서 세 가지 물리적 제약을 나열하는 설득력 있는 구조다.

koreanAlignment.en: Even the best-case scenario
would leave global energy markets undersupplied
koreanAlignment.ko: 세계 에너지 시장은 공급 부족 상태에 놓일 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: the tanker fleet that once ferried Middle Eastern crude to Asia
koreanAlignment.ko: 한때 중동 원유를 아시아로 실어 나르던 유조선단은

koreanAlignment.en: has scattered to the Atlantic

pronunciationNotes: liquefaction은 /ˌlɪkwɪˈfækʃən/ — 4음절, 두 번째에 강세 아님, 세 번째에 강세

sentences.original: Countries with substantial reserves -- Thailand, with nearly a hundred days of strategic oil stocks and seven months of import cover, or India, whose refineries are configured to process discounted Russian crude and whose power grid runs largely on domestic coal -- stand a reasonable chance of weathering the storm, whereas nations like Bangladesh, whose garment factories run on imported fuel and whose reserves barely cover three months of imports, face the grim arithmetic of simultaneous shocks to both sides of the trade balance.
sentences.translation: 상당한 비축량을 가진 나라들 — 약 100일분의 전략 석유 비축량과 7개월치 수입 충당이 가능한 태국, 혹은 할인된 러시아산 원유를 정제하도록 정유 시설이 구성되어 있고 전력망이 대부분 국내 석탄으로 가동되는 인도 — 은 위기를 견뎌낼 합리적 가능성이 있는 반면, 봉제 공장이 수입 연료로 돌아가고 외환보유액이 겨우 3개월치 수입분밖에 안 되는 방글라데시 같은 나라는 무역수지 양쪽에 동시 충격이 가해지는 냉혹한 산술에 직면한다.

grammarElements.text: Countries with substantial reserves
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: Thailand ... or India ...
grammarElements.label: 대시 안 삽입 동격구 (두 국가 예시)

grammarElements.text: stand a reasonable chance of weathering the storm
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사구

grammarElements.text: whereas nations like Bangladesh ... face the grim arithmetic
grammarElements.label: whereas 대조절

vocabulary.example: Companies with strong cash reserves have a better chance of weathering the storm.

expressions.usage: 수치적으로 피할 수 없는 어려운 상황을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Group A] stand a reasonable chance of [positive outcome], whereas [Group B] face [negative outcome]
patterns.explanation: 두 집단의 대조적 전망을 whereas로 명확히 나누는 패턴
examples: Diversified firms stand a reasonable chance of surviving, whereas single-product companies face bankruptcy.
Wealthy nations stand a reasonable chance of adapting, whereas poor ones face catastrophe.

drillQuestions.question: Countries with substantial reserves stand a reasonable chance of ______ the storm.

sentences.notes: 대시로 둘러싸인 긴 삽입구가 주어와 동사 사이에 위치하며, whereas 뒤의 대조가 문장의 핵심 메시지를 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Countries with substantial reserves
stand a reasonable chance of weathering the storm
whereas nations like Bangladesh
the grim arithmetic of simultaneous shocks

pronunciationNotes: whereas는 /weərˈæz/ — where+as로 두 번째 음절에 강세

sentences.original: The scramble for alternative LNG supplies offers little consolation, because the world's export capacity is virtually tapped out -- Australia's producers run at ninety percent, America's at ninety-five -- and the only country with meaningful spare capacity, Russia, can deliver it mainly through pipelines to Europe, a solution that would require the EU to reverse sanctions, restart mothballed infrastructure, and once again entrust its energy security to the very power it spent four years trying to escape.
sentences.translation: 대체 LNG 공급원을 향한 쟁탈전은 별다른 위안을 주지 못한다. 세계 수출 용량이 사실상 한계에 달했기 때문이다 — 호주 생산업체들은 90%로 가동되고, 미국은 95%다 — 그리고 의미 있는 여유 용량을 가진 유일한 나라인 러시아는 주로 유럽행 파이프라인을 통해서만 공급할 수 있는데, 이 해법은 EU가 제재를 철회하고 장기 보관된 기반시설을 재가동하며, 4년간 벗어나려 애쓴 바로 그 강대국에 에너지 안보를 다시 맡겨야 한다는 것을 의미한다.

grammarElements.text: The scramble for alternative LNG supplies
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: offers little consolation
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: because the world's export capacity is virtually tapped out
grammarElements.label: 이유절

grammarElements.text: a solution that would require the EU to reverse ..., restart ..., and ... entrust ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구 + require + to부정사 3개 병렬

vocabulary.example: The government considered reopening mothballed nuclear plants.
With every major supplier tapped out, prices have nowhere to go but up.

expressions.usage: 대안이 있어 보이지만 실질적 도움이 되지 않을 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Apparent solution] offers little consolation, because [reason it fails]
patterns.explanation: 겉보기에 해결책처럼 보이는 것이 실제로는 도움이 되지 않는 구조
examples: The emergency fund offers little consolation, because it covers only a fraction of the losses.
The new regulation offers little consolation, because enforcement remains weak.

drillQuestions.question: The scramble for alternative LNG supplies offers little ______, because the world's export capacity is virtually tapped out.

sentences.notes: mothball은 원래 '좀약을 넣어 보관하다'에서 온 비유로, 시설이나 프로젝트를 폐쇄하지 않고 장기 보관하는 것을 뜻한다. 문장 끝의 아이러니(탈출하려 했던 상대에게 다시 의존)가 핵심이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The scramble for alternative LNG supplies
offers little consolation
restart mothballed infrastructure
the very power it spent four years trying to escape

sentences.original: Europe's policymakers, caught between the imperative to lower energy costs for industries already losing ground to cheaper Chinese competitors and the need to preserve carbon-pricing mechanisms that drive the green transition, face a set of choices in which every path carries significant trade-offs -- extending free emission permits to energy-intensive firms undermines climate targets, while maintaining them risks accelerating the deindustrialisation of the continent's manufacturing base.
sentences.translation: 이미 더 저렴한 중국 경쟁자들에게 밀리고 있는 산업의 에너지 비용을 낮춰야 한다는 명령과 녹색 전환을 이끄는 탄소 가격 메커니즘을 보존해야 한다는 필요 사이에 끼인 유럽의 정책 입안자들은 모든 경로에 상당한 대가가 따르는 일련의 선택에 직면하고 있다 — 에너지 집약 기업에 무상 배출권을 확대하면 기후 목표가 훼손되고, 이를 유지하면 대륙 제조업 기반의 탈산업화를 가속할 위험이 있다.

grammarElements.text: Europe's policymakers
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: caught between the imperative ... and the need ...
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 삽입구 (caught between A and B)

grammarElements.text: face a set of choices in which every path carries significant trade-offs
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: extending ... undermines ... while maintaining them risks ...
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동명사 주어 대조 (A undermines X, while B risks Y)

vocabulary.example: Critics warn that high energy costs will accelerate the deindustrialisation of Europe.

expressions.usage: 상충하는 두 가지 요구 사이에서 진퇴양난인 상황을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Actor], caught between [imperative A] and [need B], faces [dilemma]
patterns.explanation: 행위자가 두 가지 상충하는 요구 사이에서 딜레마에 처한 구조
examples: The central bank, caught between the need to curb inflation and the fear of recession, chose a cautious path.
The CEO, caught between shareholder demands and employee welfare, offered a compromise.

drillQuestions.question: Europe's policymakers, caught between the imperative to lower energy costs and the need to preserve carbon-pricing mechanisms, face a set of choices in which every path carries significant ______.

sentences.notes: caught between A and B 구조가 유럽 정책 딜레마의 핵심을 압축한다. 대시 뒤에서 두 가지 선택지가 각각 다른 부작용을 낳음을 대조적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: caught between the imperative ... and the need ...
every path carries significant trade-offs
extending free emission permits
the deindustrialisation of the continent's manufacturing base

pronunciationNotes: deindustrialisation은 /diːɪnˌdʌstriəlaɪˈzeɪʃən/ — 7음절의 긴 단어, '-isation' 부분에 주 강세

sentences.original: What the crisis ultimately reveals is that the global energy system, despite a decade of investment in renewables and diversification, remains hostage to a handful of physical chokepoints and geopolitical actors, and that the transition away from fossil fuels -- however necessary -- will not proceed quickly enough to spare the world from shocks whose humanitarian toll, measured in surging fertiliser prices, record hunger, and cascading financial crises across the developing world, falls heaviest on those least responsible for the conflicts that cause them.
sentences.translation: 이번 위기가 궁극적으로 드러내는 것은, 세계 에너지 체계가 10년간의 재생에너지 투자와 다각화에도 불구하고 소수의 물리적 병목 지점과 지정학적 행위자의 인질로 남아 있다는 것, 그리고 화석연료로부터의 전환은 — 아무리 필요하더라도 — 비료 가격 급등, 기록적 기아, 개발도상국 전역의 연쇄적 금융위기로 측정되는 인도주의적 피해가 그것을 야기하는 분쟁에 가장 책임이 적은 이들에게 가장 무겁게 떨어지는 충격으로부터 세계를 구해줄 만큼 빠르게 진행되지는 않을 것이라는 점이다.

grammarElements.text: What the crisis ultimately reveals
grammarElements.label: 관계절 주어 (What절)

grammarElements.text: is that ... and that ...
grammarElements.label: be동사 + 이중 that 보어절

grammarElements.text: despite a decade of investment in renewables and diversification
grammarElements.label: 양보의 전치사구

grammarElements.text: whose humanitarian toll ... falls heaviest on those least responsible
grammarElements.label: 소유격 관계절 (whose)

vocabulary.example: The Strait of Malacca is one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints.

expressions.usage: 구조적으로 특정 요인에서 벗어나지 못하는 상황을 강하게 비유할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: What [event] ultimately reveals is that [system], despite [efforts], remains [vulnerable state]
patterns.explanation: 사건이 노력에도 불구하고 근본적 취약성을 드러낸다는 결론 패턴
examples: What the pandemic ultimately revealed is that healthcare, despite years of reform, remains dangerously underfunded.
What the crash ultimately reveals is that the banking system, despite new regulations, remains prone to the same risks.

drillQuestions.question: The global energy system remains ______ to a handful of physical chokepoints and geopolitical actors.

sentences.notes: 기사의 결론부로서 What절 주어와 이중 that절 보어가 복잡하게 얽혀 있다. 'falls heaviest on those least responsible'는 기후 정의(climate justice) 담론의 핵심 메시지를 한 구절로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What the crisis ultimately reveals
remains hostage to a handful of physical chokepoints
koreanAlignment.ko: 소수의 물리적 병목 지점의 인질로 남아 있다

koreanAlignment.en: however necessary
falls heaviest on those least responsible
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 책임이 적은 이들에게 가장 무겁게 떨어지는
